 {"id":"gt-0qki","title":"Refinery-Witness communication protocol","description":"Define mail protocol between Refinery and Witness:

FROM Witness → Refinery:
- 'Polecat ready': polecat X completed work, ready for merge
- 'Rework complete': polecat Y finished requested rework

FROM Refinery → Witness:
- 'Merge success': polecat X merged, can be cleaned up
- 'Merge failed': polecat X needs rework on <reason>
- 'Rework request': please have a polecat rebase X on current main

 {"id":"gt-17r","title":"Doctor check: Zombie session cleanup","description":"Detect and clean up zombie tmux sessions via gt doctor.

## Problem

Zombie sessions occur when:
- Agent crashes without cleanup
- gt kill fails mid-operation
- System restart leaves orphan sessions
- Session naming collision

## Checks

### ZombieSessionCheck
- List all tmux sessions matching gt-* pattern
- Cross-reference with known polecats
- Flag sessions with no corresponding polecat state
- Flag sessions for removed polecats
- Check session age vs polecat creation time

### Detection Criteria
- Session exists but polecat directory doesn't
- Session name doesn't match any registered polecat
- Polecat state=idle but session running
- Multiple sessions for same polecat

## Output

```
[WARN] Zombie tmux sessions detected:
  - gt-wyvern-OldPolecat (polecat removed)
  - gt-beads-Unknown (no matching polecat)
  - gt-wyvern-Toast (duplicate session)

  Run 'gt doctor --fix' to clean up
```

## Auto-Fix (--fix flag)

- Kill orphan tmux sessions
- Update polecat state to match reality
- Log all cleanup actions

## Safety

- Never kill sessions where polecat state=working
- Prompt before killing if --fix used without --force
- Create audit log of killed sessions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:01.446702-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:18:39.517644-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-17r","depends_on_id":"gt-f9x.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:05.66301-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-17r","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T15:44:41.945064-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-hcsz","title":"Track merge requests as beads (type=merge-request)","description":"Currently MRs are tracked in .gastown/refinery.json. For HOP audit trail and entity CV tracking, merge requests should be Beads entries with type=merge-request. This enables:
- Full audit trail of what was merged, when, by whom
- Entity chain contributions (who validated what)
- Cross-rig visibility of merge activity

The refinery would create an MR bead when work enters the queue, update status as it progresses (open → in_progress → merged/rejected).

Part of Beads-as-data-plane vision from HOP.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:07:28.836388-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T22:07:28.836388-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-hgk","title":"Mail system: message types and threading","description":"GGT mail system needs message types and threading like PGT.

## 1. Message Types
Add to internal/mail/types.go:
```go
type MessageType string
const (
    TypeTask         MessageType = "task"         // Required processing
    TypeScavenge     MessageType = "scavenge"     // Optional first-come work
    TypeNotification MessageType = "notification" // Informational
    TypeReply        MessageType = "reply"        // Response to message
)

// Update Message struct
type Message struct {
    // existing fields...
    Type     MessageType `json:"type"`
    ThreadID string      `json:"thread_id,omitempty"`
    ReplyTo  string      `json:"reply_to,omitempty"`
}
```

## 2. Priority Levels
Expand from 2 to 4:
```go
type Priority string
const (
    PriorityLow    Priority = "low"
    PriorityNormal Priority = "normal"
    PriorityHigh   Priority = "high"
    PriorityUrgent Priority = "urgent"
)
```

## 3. CLI Updates
internal/cmd/mail.go:
- Add --type flag to send: `gt mail send ... --type task`
- Add --reply-to flag: `gt mail send ... --reply-to <msg-id>`
- Add thread command: `gt mail thread <thread-id>`

## 4. Threading Logic
NewMessage() should auto-generate thread_id if not a reply.
Reply messages inherit thread_id from original.

## Files to Modify
- internal/mail/types.go: Add types, expand Priority
- internal/mail/mailbox.go: Thread filtering
- internal/cmd/mail.go: CLI flags and thread command

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/mail/message.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Messages have type field (default: notification)
- [ ] 4 priority levels supported
- [ ] Reply creates thread with shared thread_id
- [ ] gt mail thread <id> shows conversation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:46:55.29463-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T20:14:28.308997-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T20:14:28.308997-08:00"}

2. Consider simplifying pinToHook() if the pinned field becomes reliable
3. Update gt-o3is to reference the fix

## Blocked By

This issue is blocked by external:beads:pinned-field-fix (bd-phtv).
When that capability is shipped, this issue becomes ready.

 {"id":"gt-htto","title":"Heartbeat convention: simple liveness signal for agents","description":"Lightweight liveness signal extracted from Deacon epic (gt-5af).

**Implementation**: Each agent writes a timestamp file on activity:
```bash
echo '{"ts":"'$(date -Iseconds)'"}' > ~/gt/<role>/heartbeat.json
```

**Integration points**:
- SessionStart hook writes heartbeat
- Periodic activity (mail check, work completion) refreshes it
- `gt status` shows staleness (e.g., 'mayor: 5m ago')

**Weight**: ~5 lines per agent
**Value**: Quick debugging - see which agents are active at a glance

No monitoring daemon needed - human checks `gt status` when curious.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:40:45.459903-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:40:45.459903-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-jzot","title":"gt done: Notify Witness with exit outcome","description":"When polecat runs gt done, it should send mail to Witness:

```
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s 'POLECAT_DONE <name>' -m 'Exit: COMPLETED
Issue: <issue-id>
MR: <mr-id>
Branch: <branch>'
```

Exit types:
- COMPLETED: Work done, MR submitted
- ESCALATED: Hit blocker, needs human
- DEFERRED: Work paused, issue still open

This enables Witness patrol to:
1. See completion in inbox-check step
2. Verify git state is clean
3. Kill session and prune worktree
4. Close the polecat lease in its patrol wisp

Paired with gt-r6td (spawn notification) - together they bracket polecat lifecycle.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/nux","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:31:31.266716-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:55:15.215207-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:55:15.215207-08:00","close_reason":"Closed"}

 {"id":"gt-k1lr","title":"Consolidate configuration architecture: three-tier model","description":"Rationalize Gas Town's scattered configuration into a clean three-tier model:

## Current Problems
- Config split across mayor/, .gastown/, and rig/config.json
- Runtime state mixed with configuration
- Hidden directories (.gastown/) are missed by agents
- Category confusion: identity vs config vs runtime state

## Proposed Architecture

### Tier 1: Town Config (mayor/)
```
mayor/
├── town.json      # Town identity (unchanged)
├── rigs.json      # Rig registry (unchanged)
└── config.json    # NEW: Town-level configuration
    # theme defaults, daemon settings, etc.
```

### Tier 2: Town Runtime (.runtime/)
```
~/gt/.runtime/                    # NEW: Gitignored
├── daemon.json                   # {pid, started_at, heartbeat}
├── deacon.json                   # {cycle, last_action}
└── agent-requests.json           # Lifecycle requests
```

### Tier 3: Rig Config (settings/)
```
<rig>/
├── config.json                   # Rig identity only (type, git_url, beads.prefix)
├── settings/                     # NEW: Visible, git-tracked
│   ├── config.json               # theme, merge_queue, max_workers
│   ├── namepool.json             # pool settings (style, max)
│   └── roles/                    # Per-role overrides (optional)
└── .runtime/                     # NEW: Gitignored
    ├── witness.json              # {state, started_at, stats}
    ├── refinery.json             # {state, started_at, stats}
    └── namepool-state.json       # {in_use, overflow_next}
```

## Key Principles
1. **Visible > Hidden** for config agents need to find
2. **Git-tracked** for identity and config; **gitignored** for runtime
3. **Separation of concerns**: identity, config, runtime are distinct
4. **Locality**: town config at town root, rig config at rig root

## Migration
- Move .gastown/ contents to appropriate new locations
- Update all code that reads/writes these files
- Update .gitignore patterns
- Update documentation (architecture.md, CLAUDE.md)

-{"id":"gt-o3is","title":"gt sling pinToHook doesn't set pinned boolean field","description":"## Bug

When `gt sling` pins work to an agent's hook via subprocess, `bd pin` reports success but the `pinned` boolean field is NOT persisted.

## Root Cause (Found 2025-12-23)

The issue is a sync/flush timing problem:

1. `gt sling` calls `pinToHook()` which runs `bd --no-daemon pin`
2. `bd pin` updates the SQLite database with `pinned=1`
3. `bd pin` schedules a flush to JSONL (via FlushManager)
4. Subprocess exits BEFORE flush completes
5. `gt sling` then calls `syncBeads()` which runs `bd sync`
6. `bd sync` imports from JSONL which DOESN'T have pinned field
7. Import overwrites SQLite, resetting `pinned=0`

The assignee field IS set correctly because it's also in the database before sync, but sync imports the JSONL which doesn't have the pinned field (due to omitempty on false value).

## Reproduction

```bash
# Create issue
bd create "test" --json

# Sling it
gt sling <id> gastown/crew/max

# Check - pinned is null!
bd show <id> --json | jq '.[0].pinned'
```

## Fix Options

1. **Make bd pin synchronous**: Force flush before subprocess exit
2. **Skip sync after pin**: Don't call syncBeads after pinToHook
3. **Use direct DB update in gt**: Call UpdateIssue directly instead of bd subprocess
4. **Fix flush-on-exit**: Ensure PersistentPostRun waits for flush completion

## Workaround

The handoff bead attachment (`AttachMolecule`) is the primary mechanism for tracking what's on an agent's hook. The pinned field is cosmetic for `bd hook` visibility.

## Files

- gastown: internal/cmd/sling.go (slingToCrew, pinToHook, syncBeads)
- gastown: internal/beads/beads.go (Pin method)
- beads: cmd/bd/pin.go (bd pin command)
- beads: cmd/bd/flush_manager.go (async flush)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:41:12.668958-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:03:22.343937-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-o3is","title":"gt sling pinToHook doesn't set pinned boolean field","description":"## Updated Root Cause Analysis (2025-12-23)

The issue is **NOT** in gt as originally thought. It's in the beads (bd) codebase.

### What happens:
1. `bd pin` correctly sets pinned=1 in SQLite
2. Any subsequent `bd` command (even `bd show`) resets pinned to 0
3. This happens even with `--no-auto-import` and `--sandbox` flags

### Evidence:
```bash
$ bd --no-daemon pin gt-k08o --for=max
📌 Pinned gt-k08o to max's hook

$ sqlite3 beads.db 'SELECT id, pinned FROM issues WHERE id="gt-k08o"'
gt-k08o|1   # ← Correct immediately after pin

$ bd --no-daemon --no-auto-import show gt-k08o
[shows issue without pinned field]

$ sqlite3 beads.db 'SELECT id, pinned FROM issues WHERE id="gt-k08o"'  
gt-k08o|0   # ← WRONG\! bd show overwrote it
```

### Where to look:
The bug is likely in one of these beads code paths:
- Some import/hydration running despite --no-auto-import
- WAL mode not flushing before subsequent reads
- Multi-repo or redirect handling corrupting pinned field

### Workaround:
The handoff bead attachment mechanism (AttachMolecule) works correctly.
The pinned field is cosmetic for `bd hook` visibility only.
gt sling correctly uses AttachMolecule for work assignment.

### Next steps:
Create a beads issue to fix this properly in the bd codebase.
This is not a gt issue.","notes":"Created beads bug: bd-phtv in ~/gt/beads","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:41:12.668958-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:32:43.506087-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-odvf","title":"Document bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI","description":"Create CLI reference documentation for molecule commands:

## bd mol bond

Instantiate a proto into a runnable molecule.

```bash
bd mol bond <proto-id> [--wisp] [--assignee=<addr>]
```

- Default: creates a Mol (durable, in main beads)
- --wisp: creates a Wisp (ephemeral, in .beads-ephemeral/)
- --assignee: who will execute this molecule

## bd mol squash

Complete a molecule and generate digest.

```bash
bd mol squash <mol-id> --summary='...'
```

- For Mol: creates digest in git history
- For Wisp: evaporates (no permanent record)
- --summary: required summary of what was accomplished

## bd mol burn

Abandon a molecule without completing.

```bash
bd mol burn <mol-id> [--reason='...']
```

- Discards molecule state
- No digest created
- Use when molecule is no longer needed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:06.462105-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T02:14:23.810794-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T02:14:23.810794-08:00","close_reason":"Already documented in docs/molecules.md lines 70-235","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-odvf","depends_on_id":"gt-62hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:17.530156-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-ynp6","title":"gt context --usage: estimate remaining context via tmux line count","description":"## Summary

Implement `gt context --usage` to estimate remaining context capacity.

## Approach

Use tmux scrollback buffer line count as proxy for token usage:
- `tmux capture-pane -p | wc -l` gives current line count
- Heuristics (tunable per model):
  - Opus 4.5: ~1800 lines = warning threshold
  - ~2000+ lines = critical, need handoff

## Output Format
```
Context Usage: 1642 lines (~82%)
Status: WARNING - consider handoff soon
```

## Notes
- Heuristics are model-specific, need config
- Line count is proxy, not exact token count
- Good enough for autonomous patrol decisions","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:19:13.108332-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T01:19:13.108332-08:00"}
+{"id":"gt-yt6g","title":"Standardize session end: gt handoff for all roles","description":"## Summary

Standardize session ending across all agent roles to use `gt handoff` as the canonical command. This is critical for the beads propulsion cycle - turning agent sessions from pets into cattle.

## Current State (Inconsistent)

| Role | Current Guidance | Command |
|------|-----------------|---------|
| Mayor | Manual mail send | `gt mail send mayor/ -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Crew | Manual mail send | `gt mail send <rig>/crew/<name> -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Witness | Manual mail send | `gt mail send <rig>/witness -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Refinery | Manual mail send | `gt mail send <rig>/refinery -s 'HANDOFF:...' -m '...'` |
| Deacon | Exit on high context | (implicit) |
| Polecat | `gt done` | `gt done [--exit TYPE]` |

## Target State (Unified)

All roles use `gt handoff`:
- `gt handoff` - Hand off current session to fresh instance
- `gt handoff -s 'context' -m 'details'` - Hand off with custom message
- For polecats: `gt handoff` internally calls `gt done`

## Changes Required

### 1. Code Changes
- [ ] Update `gt handoff` to detect polecat role and call `gt done` internally
- [ ] Consider adding `--exit` flag to `gt handoff` for polecat compatibility

### 2. CLAUDE.md Updates (gastown)
- [ ] ~/gt/CLAUDE.md (Mayor)
- [ ] gastown/crew/max/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] gastown/crew/joe/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] gastown/witness/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] gastown/refinery/CLAUDE.md (and rig/)
- [ ] deacon/CLAUDE.md

### 3. CLAUDE.md Updates (beads)
- [ ] beads/mayor/rig/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/crew/dave/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/crew/zoey/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/witness/CLAUDE.md
- [ ] beads/refinery/CLAUDE.md (and rig/)

### 4. Architecture Docs
- [ ] docs/patrol-system-design.md
- [ ] gastown/mayor/rig/docs/prompts.md
- [ ] gastown/mayor/rig/docs/session-management.md

## New Session End Checklist (Universal)

```
# SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL

[ ] 1. git status              (check uncommitted changes)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m '...'     (commit with issue ID)
[ ] 4. bd sync                 (sync beads)
[ ] 5. git push                (push to remote - CRITICAL)
[ ] 6. gt handoff              (hand off to fresh session)
       OR gt handoff -s 'Context' -m 'Details for next session'
```

## Why This Matters

The handoff mechanism is what turns agent sessions from **pets** (precious, long-lived) into **cattle** (disposable, replaceable). At any time, any agent can:
1. Send itself a detailed handoff mail (or sling itself a mol)
2. System shuts them down and restarts them
3. Fresh session runs priming and reads mail
4. Work continues seamlessly

This enables:
- Unlimited context through automatic cycling
- Clean recovery from any state
- Consistent behavior across all roles
- Simplified agent instructions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:57:25.246279-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:57:25.246279-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-yx4","title":"Town root .beads has schema mismatch with bd","description":"The .beads directory at town root (/Users/stevey/gt/.beads) has an incompatible schema:

```
Error: failed to open database: failed to initialize schema: sqlite3: SQL logic error: no such column: thread_id
```

Meanwhile, gastown/.beads (symlinked to mayor/rig/.beads) works fine.

## Impact

- gt mail inbox fails at town root
- gt handoff sends mail to broken db
- Daemon can't check its inbox

## Options

1. Delete town root .beads/beads.db and let it recreate
2. Symlink town root .beads to gastown/.beads
3. Run schema migration on existing db

## Root Cause

Likely a beads version upgrade that added thread_id column, but the town root db was created before that.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:31:35.559042-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T00:39:32.211083-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T00:39:32.211083-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-z3qf","title":"Overhaul gt mol to match bd mol chemistry interface","description":"## The Sling: Unified Work Dispatch

This issue tracks the overhaul of `gt molecule` to align with chemistry metaphor and introduce the **Universal Gas Town Propulsion Principle**.

### The Propulsion Principle

> **If you find something on your hook, YOU RUN IT.**

This is the one rule that drives all Gas Town agents.

### The Sling Operation

`gt sling <thing> <target> [options]` - unified command for spawn + assign + pin.

See: `gastown/mayor/rig/docs/sling-design.md`

### Implementation Tasks

| Issue | Title | Priority |
|-------|-------|----------|
| gt-4ev4 | Implement gt sling command | P1 |
| gt-uym5 | Implement gt mol status command | P1 |
| gt-i4kq | Update templates for Propulsion Principle | P1 |
| gt-7hor | Document the Propulsion Principle | P2 |

### Command Changes

| Old | New |
|-----|-----|
| `gt molecule instantiate` | `gt sling` |
| `gt molecule attach` | `gt sling --force` |
| `gt molecule detach` | `gt mol burn` |
| `gt molecule progress` | `gt mol status` |
| `gt molecule list` | `gt mol catalog` |
| `gt spawn --molecule` | `gt sling` |

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `gt sling` works for protos, issues, and epics
- [ ] `gt mol status` shows hook state
- [ ] Templates updated for propulsion principle
- [ ] Old commands deprecated with warnings
- [ ] Documentation complete","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T03:02:38.049324-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:37:37.562677-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:37:37.562677-08:00","close_reason":"Core sling work complete (gt-4ev4, gt-uym5, gt-7hor closed). gt-i4kq (template updates) remains open but is independent polish work.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-z3qf","depends_on_id":"gt-4ev4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T12:10:42.394653-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-z3qf","depends_on_id":"gt-uym5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T12:10:42.46834-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-z3qf","depends_on_id":"gt-i4kq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T12:10:42.541384-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-z3qf","depends_on_id":"gt-7hor","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T12:10:42.613099-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
